what_it_does: Bash script that, after a GPU restart, calls a local qwen3tts service on :4128 to synthesize a reference WAV from a text description of a designed voice and saves it as COMPANY_VOICE_REF so clone engines have a real clip to clone.
observations: Uses python json.dumps to safely encode REF_TEXT and REF_DESC into the POST payload
Writes the WAV directly from curl to REF_PATH and validates HTTP 200 and file size > 44 bytes
Defaults REF_PATH, REF_TEXT, and REF_DESC via environment-variable fallbacks
Idempotent: can be re-run to regenerate with a tweaked description
Runs only post-restart because qwen3tts needs the GPU loaded
standouts: Treats Qwen3-VoiceDesign as the IDENTITY SOURCE: it designs the voice from text and produces the canonical reference clip, which clone engines then clone
Avoids any human recording or licensed audio by synthesizing the reference from a persona description
Embodies the trial concept of 'same woman underneath, five souls on top' by sharing one reference across character variations
conventions: Uses bash strict mode set -euo pipefail
Environment overrides with default values using ${VAR:-default}
Health-checks the dependent service before use
Validates the produced artifact size as a quick sanity check
Uses curl -w %{http_code} for explicit HTTP status handling
concerns: Hardcodes QWEN_URL to http://127.0.0.1:4128 with no configuration override
On HTTP failure the WAV file is overwritten with the error response before the script exits
stat -c%s is Linux-specific and may fail on macOS/BSD
Only checks WAV byte count, not actual audio validity or duration
Single 300s timeout with no retry/backoff for a potentially slow first synthesis
notes: Post-restart operational step that bootstraps the entire voice stack by minting a synthetic reference clip from a designed persona description.
questions: What exact JSON schema and field names does qwen3tts /tts expect for the voice description?
How is the default REF_DESC kept in sync with personas.VOICE_BASE?
What happens if qwen3tts is up on /health but the model is not fully loaded?
How do the clone engines learn the generated REF_PATH — via voice.env or runtime injection?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to synthesize a designed reference clip after restart, and that is exactly what the script does.
apparent_themes: Shared canonical reference for downstream cloning
intention_signals: The script is trying to create a single, consistent synthetic voice identity without human recordings by using a design-capable TTS model as the root source of timbre.
novelty: It uses a text-to-voice-design model to generate the very reference clip that traditional clone engines require, flipping the usual 'record a human first' workflow.
contribution: Provides a self-contained bootstrapping operation that converts a text voice description into a shared reference WAV, giving downstream clone engines a designed starting point rather than a recorded one.
summary_for_embedding: Bash script that calls a local qwen3tts service to synthesize a reference WAV from a text voice description and saves it as COMPANY_VOICE_REF for clone engines.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a post-restart operational step to generate the canonical synthetic reference voice clip from a designed persona description.
